sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Mr. Jackson, a 45-year-old male, presents with right elbow pain that has been ongoing for three weeks, starting after increasing his tennis activity.
He describes the pain as an 8/10 when using his arm and 3/10 when at rest, with a sharp, stabbing, and throbbing sensation.
The pain is localized to the outside part of his elbow and does not radiate to his arm or shoulder.
He has tried taking Tylenol and ibuprofen, but they provide only temporary relief.
He has not noticed any improvement with ice application.
He has a history of playing tennis in his younger years and was a state champion in high school.
Past medical history: Mr. Jackson has a history of diabetes type 2, mild intermittent asthma, a history of penicillin allergy, and a surgical history of gallbladder removal 10 years ago.
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications:
Metformin 500 milligrams twice a day for diabetes
Flovent  twice a day for asthma
Albuterol as needed for asthma
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The physical exam is largely unremarkable, except for moderate lateral epicondylar tenderness of the right elbow, which worsens with passive range of motion and palpation.
There is mild pain with resisted extension of the right wrist.
The cardiovascular exam is normal, with a grade 3/6 systolic ejection murmur.
The musculoskeletal exam is otherwise normal.
Test Results:
Right elbow x-ray: No acute fracture or bony abnormality, no malalignment, and no joint effusion.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: Mr. Jackson has acute lateral epicondylitis of his right elbow, also known as tennis elbow, likely due to overuse from increased tennis activity.
His diabetes type 2 is out of control, and his asthma is under good control.
Problem list: Diabetes type 2; Acute lateral epicondylitis of the right elbow; Mild intermittent asthma
Plan:
I prescribed Ultram 50 milligrams once a day for pain management of his right elbow.
I want him to rest his right arm as much as possible, avoid repetitive stress, and ice the right elbow for 20 minutes, 3 times a day.
I also ordered a hemoglobin A1c level, a CBC blood test, and a chem-12 blood test to assess his diabetes control.
I increased his metformin to 1000 milligrams twice a day.
I will also prescribe a refill of Albuterol as needed for asthma.
Follow-up: I will see Mr. Jackson in 4 weeks to reevaluate his elbow and adjust his treatment plan as needed.
